content.claim: Students from higher socioeconomic backgrounds are significantly more likely to attend early-selective academic tracks even when prior test scores are controlled for, and track placement correlates strongly with parental education rather than measured ability alone.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Study controls for previous test scores but cannot fully isolate motivation or cultural factors driving selection
Based on Hungarian domestic data; cross-national generalizability unclear
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
